methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $43M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Tevel Aerobotics is a Series B-stage company in the Drones & Autonomous sector that has raised $42.6M to date. The relevant comparable universe consists of 8 private companies operating in this sector, including Elroy Air, Zipline, Joby Aviation, Nuro, and Archer Aviation. No public comps with sufficient comparability exist in this nascent market.

Given data constraints, a single valuation method was applied. The Stage-Progression method uses the $42.6M in total capital raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B to subsequent-stage progression, yielding an implied valuation of $81M. A Peer-Median approach was not viable; while 5 same-sector peers exist, insufficient datapoints met narrow filtering criteria (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation tier), and an all-sector median would lack sufficient precision.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $69M (low) to $93M (high), with a median estimate of $81M. This range reflects ±15% variance around the stage-progression result, a conservative band given the reliance on a single method. Key uncertainties include the absence of directly comparable Series B precedents in the Drones & Autonomous space, the nascency of commercial autonomous systems markets, and limited visibility into Tevel's revenue or unit economics relative to the peer set. Additional data on revenue multiples or customer traction would materially strengthen conviction in this range.
